examples.title: Algorithm: AO* Search
examples.description: Pseudocode for the AO* algorithm, an extension of A* search for AND-OR graphs.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AO*
INPUT: AND-OR graph G, start node s
OUTPUT: Solution graph or failure

BEGIN
 Initialize graph G with start node s
 Set h(s) = heuristic estimate
 Repeat until s is SOLVED or h(s) = infinity:
 Select a non-terminal leaf node n from the best partial solution graph rooted at s
 Expand n, generating its successors
 For each new successor m:
 If m is a terminal (solved) node, mark SOLVED, h(m) = 0
 Else set h(m) = heuristic estimate
 Propagate cost revisions backward:
 Update h-values of ancestors based on new information
 For AND nodes: h = sum of successor costs + arc costs
 For OR nodes: h = min of successor costs + arc cost
 Mark nodes as SOLVED when appropriate
 If s is SOLVED, extract and return the solution graph
 If h(s) = infinity, return failure
END
examples.explanation: The AO* algorithm extends A* search to handle AND-OR graphs by iteratively expanding nodes and propagating cost estimates backward.

examples.title: Python: AO* Search Implementation
examples.description: A Python implementation of the AO* algorithm.
examples.explanation: This Python implementation provides the basic structure for the AO* algorithm, including node representation, cost updates, and solution extraction.
